assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes specific command to check deprecated API usage: 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention admission webhooks at all, which are a common cause of API latency and 503 errors after upgrades

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes section on 'Resource quota/limits recalculation' with commands to check node resource capacity and resource quota usage

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple commands to check kube-system pods: API server pods, CoreDNS pods, and CNI pods with specific kubectl commands

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_duration_seconds' command in the monitoring section

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes command to check network policies: 'kubectl get networkpolicies -A' and discusses CNI/networking configuration changes

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check for pod events including 'kubectl get events -A --field-selector reason=FailedScheduling' and readiness probe failures

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes dedicated 'HPA/VPA behavior changes' section with commands to check HPA status and scaling events
